title: Instrumental — Means & Accompaniment
description: Master the instrumental case (7th case): s kým/čím? for accompaniment, instrumental endings across genders, transport expressions (autem, vlakem, pěšky), professions with pracovat jako, and instrumental time expressions.

data.explanation: 's kým?' = with whom? It's the instrumental case question for people. 's čím?' = with what? (for things). Both use 's' (or 'se') + instrumental case.

exercises.prompt: Which is the correct instrumental form of 'auto' (car)?

data.explanation: 'Autem' = by car (instrumental of 'auto'). Neuter nouns ending in -o take -em in instrumental: auto → autem, město → městem. This is how you express means of transport in Czech.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct means of transport in instrumental.

data.sentence: Jedu do práce ___ (autem). Bratr jezdí ___ (vlakem). Sestra chodí ___ (pěšky).
data.explanation: 'Autem' = by car, 'vlakem' = by train (both instrumental). 'Pěšky' = on foot (adverb, not instrumental — an exception!). Transport expressions use instrumental for vehicles: letadlem (by plane), metrem (by metro), autobusem (by bus).

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'I go to work by car.'

data.source: I go to work by car.
data.grammar_note: 'Jedu' = I go (by vehicle). 'do práce' = to work. 'autem' = by car (instrumental of 'auto'). The verb 'jet' (to go by vehicle) pairs with instrumental means of transport.

exercises.prompt: Which phrase means 'I work as a teacher' in Czech?

data.question_cz: Jak řeknete 'I work as a teacher'?
data.question_en: How do you say 'I work as a teacher'?
data.explanation: 'Pracuju jako učitel.' = I work as a teacher. 'Jako' + nominative (not instrumental!) is used for professions with 'pracovat'. The instrumental alone can also express this in more formal Czech: 'Pracuju učitelem.' Both are correct.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the instrumental form of these masculine nouns.

data.sentence: Jdu s ___ (kamarád). Mluvím s ___ (bratr). Jedu s ___ (učitel).
data.explanation: Masculine instrumental ending: -em. 'Kamarád → kamarádem', 'bratr → bratrem' (note the fleeting -e-), 'učitel → učitelem'. Soft-stem masculine nouns take -em as well: 'muž → mužem', 'stroj → strojem'.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the instrumental form of these feminine nouns.

data.sentence: Jdu s ___ (kamarádka). Mluvím s ___ (učitelka). Cestuju s ___ (máma).
data.explanation: Feminine instrumental ending: -ou. 'Kamarádka → kamarádkou', 'učitelka → učitelkou', 'máma → mámou'. Soft-stem feminine nouns take -í: 'ulice → ulicí'. This is one of the most distinctive Czech case endings.

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'I'm going to the cinema with a friend (female).'

data.source: I'm going to the cinema with a friend (female).
data.grammar_note: 'Jdu' = I go (on foot). 's kamarádkou' = with a friend (female) — instrumental of 'kamarádka'. 'do kina' = to the cinema. 'S' + instrumental is the standard accompaniment pattern.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'a year ago' in Czech?

data.question_en: How do you say 'a year ago' in Czech?
data.explanation: 'Před rokem' = a year ago. 'Před' + instrumental expresses 'ago' or 'before' in time expressions: před týdnem (a week ago), před měsícem (a month ago), před hodinou (an hour ago).

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct instrumental time expression.

data.sentence: Před ___ (rok) jsem byl v Praze. Před ___ (týden) jsem mluvil s Janou. Před ___ (měsíc) jsem začal novou práci.
data.explanation: 'Před' + instrumental for time expressions: 'před rokem' (a year ago), 'před týdnem' (a week ago), 'před měsícem' (a month ago). All use the instrumental ending -em for masculine nouns.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form: 'I go to work by car.'

data.explanation: 'Jedu do práce autem.' = I go to work by car. Word order: verb (jedu) + destination (do práce) + means (autem). 'Autem' is instrumental of 'auto'.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear (instrumental accompaniment).

data.note: 'Jdu s kamarádem do kina.' = I'm going to the cinema with a friend (male). 's kamarádem' = with a friend (instrumental of 'kamarád'). 'do kina' = to the cinema.
